businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and enterprise AI data analytics platform leader, built on three pillars: Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise. FY2025 revenue $4.48B (+60%). US commercial revenue exceeded $650M (Q4 +137% YoY). Pentagon adopted Maven AI as an official program of record for military-wide deployment. Selected as Golden Dome $185B missile defense software developer. Palantir tools are in active combat use in the Iran war per CNBC.
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) - corporate data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - generative AI integration platform
AIP Bootcamps - rapid adoption programs driving customer conversion

keyMetrics.name: Trailing P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: 7.4x the S&P 500 IT sector avg of 34x. Motley Fool notes earnings must grow 5x (+489%) to bring P/E to 50x

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS estimate of $1.22. Still 3x the growth stock avg of 30-40x

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $4.48B to FY2026 $7.40B projected. Iran war expansion could further accelerate defense AI demand

keyMetrics.name: P/S Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: 5-7x premium vs software sector avg 10-15x. Still in overvaluation territory vs growth

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: -31% from 52-week high of $207. Defense tailwinds outweighed by tech selloff pressure

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Neutral zone. Room before oversold but weak bounce momentum in downtrend

growthDrivers.title: Stellantis 5-year partnership renewal and expansion (3/30, today)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir announced renewal of its long-standing partnership with Stellantis (world's 4th largest automaker) for 5 more years. Validates Foundry platform's enterprise value and demonstrates customer expansion

growthDrivers.title: CNBC: 'Iran war is defense tech's opportunity, Palantir tools in active use' (3/28)
growthDrivers.description: CNBC reports Palantir has billions in Pentagon contracts and CEO Karp confirmed tools are used in active combat. Houthi entry expands demand further

growthDrivers.title: Motley Fool: 'Palantir's blockbuster week' with 3 key developments (3/29)
growthDrivers.description: Golden Dome, Maven adoption, and Polymarket partnership named as three blockbuster developments in a single week

growthDrivers.title: Golden Dome $185B missile defense software (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir and Anduril selected for Trump admin's $185B Golden Dome missile defense system software. Houthi missile attacks make missile defense more urgent

growthDrivers.title: Polymarket AI fraud prevention partnership (3/26)
growthDrivers.description: Vergence AI handling real-time trading surveillance and fraud detection for Polymarket. Motley Fool calls it a 'sports betting watchdog' expansion into financial services

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI official program of record (3/23)
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon designated Maven Smart System as official program of record for military-wide, long-term deployment. Combat-tested in Iran war

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI, highest security clearances, Maven military adoption, Iran war combat validation. Moats money cannot buy

moats.description: Ontology platform deeply integrated into government/military systems. Replacement costs are prohibitive
Highest-level clearances and 20+ years of trust. Virtually impossible for new entrants to achieve
Real-time targeting in Iran war. Houthi entry expands combat footprint and demand
AIP bootcamp rapid adoption and Ontology data model ecosystem expansion

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup. Golden Dome partner with Palantir. $60B valuation

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competition. Far behind in government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants entering government AI. Lack Ontology-depth integration

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Significantly behind in revenue and growth rate

recentNews.headline: Stellantis 5-year partnership renewal and expansion announced
recentNews.significance: Global 4th largest automaker renews long-term partnership. Validates Foundry enterprise value and customer retention

recentNews.headline: Houthi rebels fire missiles at Israel, Iran war new front. Defense AI demand grows
recentNews.significance: Houthi entry expands the war. CNBC reports Palantir tools in active combat use. Golden Dome missile defense urgency increases

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: Palantir AI becoming a 'sports betting watchdog'
recentNews.significance: Deep analysis of Vergence AI's Polymarket fraud detection. Defense surveillance tech expanding into commercial applications

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'Palantir blockbuster week, 3 things shareholders must know'
recentNews.significance: Golden Dome, Maven, Polymarket as three blockbuster developments. Also presents bearish '5 years same price' argument

recentNews.headline: UNICOM Systems adds 27,000 PLTR shares
recentNews.significance: UNICOM grew position by 3.3% in Q4 (838,992 shares). Institutional buying continues

analystOpinions.notableComment: Iran war expansion + Stellantis 5-year renewal = defense/enterprise dual tailwind. But P/E 250x is the valuation ceiling. Motley Fool offers both 'blockbuster' praise and '5 years same price' skepticism

risks.title: Extreme valuation, '5 years same price' bear case
risks.description: P/E 250x (7.4x IT sector avg). Motley Fool calculates earnings must grow 489% to bring P/E to 50x. War tailwinds already priced in

risks.title: Oil $100, macro tech selloff
risks.description: Houthi entry, oil $100+, inflation resurgence. Defense beneficiary but high-valuation tech selloff pressure dominates

risks.title: Below both 50-day and 200-day SMAs
risks.description: Below 50-day ($149) and 200-day ($169). Three consecutive down days, weekly -5.1%. $126 support level open

risks.title: Analyst opinion split
risks.description: 17 Buy, 14 Hold, 5 Sell. Market skepticism on valuation persists

risks.title: Stock-based compensation dilution
risks.description: Share count growing 4.67% annually. Ongoing SBC dilutes existing shareholder value

buyReasons.title: Stellantis 5-year renewal + Iran war: defense/enterprise dual tailwind
buyReasons.rationale: Today's Stellantis renewal (enterprise) plus CNBC-confirmed combat use (defense). Demand growing on both fronts simultaneously

buyReasons.title: Blockbuster week: Golden Dome + Maven + Polymarket
buyReasons.rationale: Golden Dome $185B (Phase 1 'many billions'), Maven military adoption, Polymarket AI partnership. Three major developments in one week

buyReasons.title: -31% from high, fundamentals at all-time best
buyReasons.rationale: 52-week high $207 to $143, -31%. But fundamentals are the strongest ever with war expansion and Stellantis renewal

buyReasons.title: Institutional buying continues
buyReasons.rationale: Czech central bank, Spire Wealth, UNICOM Systems all adding positions steadily

overallOpinion: Today's Stellantis 5-year renewal proves enterprise AI expansion continues. Houthi entry expands the Iran war, growing defense AI demand. Both defense and enterprise tailwinds simultaneously is rare

But Motley Fool simultaneously calls it 'blockbuster' and predicts '5 years same price.' P/E 250x needs 489% earnings growth to normalize. Even Stellantis and war tailwinds are already priced in at this valuation
Oil $100 and inflation resurgence pressure all high-valuation tech. Defense beneficiary, but P/S 76x cannot escape macro selloff
Consensus $199 (+39%). -31% from high. Stellantis, Golden Dome, Maven, Polymarket make fundamentals the strongest ever. Hold, small dollar-cost averaging only

investmentVerdict.summary: Stellantis 5-year renewal confirms enterprise traction. Iran war expansion (Houthi entry) grows defense demand. Dual tailwinds yet P/E 250x prices everything in. Oil $100 macro headwinds pressure high-valuation tech. Small dollar-cost averaging only
